@everyone Hardware/Mechanical Team began work on hull design for Phase 2 tonight. <@874955949725589554>  put his Fusion-360 skills to good use and modeled out some end-caps and an electronics tray to retrofit a 6" schedule-80 PVC pipe into a water-tight electronics case for our hull. The tray is of appropriate dimensions that it would be able to fit a Jetson or Pi5, and up to 6 ESCs with room for batteries and cable management. For hull we will present a form factor idea Thursday night that would allow us to use a similar design to the current one, but with provisions for more degrees of control than are available with the SeaPerch kit. We've also been using this thread to keep track of components for hull construction and hardware implementation. You can see the direction we have in mind with some of the components as well. If you know of better products that would be more suitable, don't hesitate to share links here as well. Hull design team will meet again Thursday prior to the meeting at 5:30 to hash out final specs on hull design for presentation at the group meeting.

@everyone Had two leaks in the hull during preliminary wet test. One on the gasket for the lid and another around the wires of a gland/bulkhead. Was the lid seal leak at the union of the silicone gasket?  If so, cut the joint out and join it with something softer than super glue and it should be fine
I didn’t look that close at where that was coming through. I’ll take a look tomorrow before we start moving things from one hull to the next
Yes. It was from the union. I couldn’t remember if it was superglue.

I joined it with super glue just to see if it would work and it did, but it definitely did put a hard spot in the gasket where a gap can form.  Two solutions are to cut that out and rejoin with silicone or just reposition the joint near a lid screw, where the clamping force is highest

@everyone I’m not sure what pins were originally set for the PWM GPIO pins, but arbitrarily the current configuration with the DuPont connectors is GPIO 22 & 24 are the vertical thrusters.
